headline: Explosion in Monaco targets Ukrainian oligarch Vadim Ermolaev
description: The homemade bomb that exploded on Monday evening in front of a building in the principality apparently targeted Ermolaev, a wealthy Ukrainian businessman. Although he had renounced his nationality, he still maintained opaque business activities connected to his country of origin.

Spain has three months to review one million applications for migrant regularization
description: Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez's government is defending a measure aimed at supporting economic growth amid a 'demographic winter.' This is Spain's seventh such initiative in 40 years. Initially, the plan was met with broad consensus, but it has since become a source of division.

headline: Global oceans experience record heat in June, driven by El Niño and climate change
description: The average surface temperature reached 20.98°C in June, surpassing the previous 2024 high, according to the European observatory Copernicus Marine. The first half of 2026 was the second-warmest on record.

headline: Mbappé fires France into World Cup last 16
description: France's victory over Sweden on Tuesday has elevated prolific striker Kylian Mbappé to an incredible World Cup record:  18 in 18 matches – only one behind Argentina's Lionel Messi's all-time record of 19 goals.

headline: EU judges' opaque handling of potential conflicts of interest
description: Members of the Court of Justice of the European Union have ruled on cases involving companies or economic sectors in which they had interests, according to an investigation conducted since 2024 by Investigate Europe and its partners, including Le Monde.

'The King and the Mockingbird': The animated masterpiece that inspired Studio Ghibli
description: His film took more than three decades to reach the screen. In 1946, Paul Grimault, then 41, began work on 'Le Roi et l'Oiseau' ('The King and the Mockingbird') with Jacques Prévert writing the screenplay. He finally saw it screened in 1979, at 74. The hand-drawn animation became a cult classic, inspiring generations of viewers and filmmakers.

headline: At the Four Seasons in Damascus, power shifts but the elite remain
description: A symbol of Bashar al-Assad's grip on Syria, the luxury hotel was the scene of lavish parties and business dealings until the fall of the dictator in December 2024. After briefly serving as the headquarters for the new leaders, it is now a networking hub for entrepreneurs seeking business opportunities in a country where everything must be rebuilt.

headline: 'Their mouths, eyes, hands, were taped and they were thrown into the hole': Russian soldiers confront Putin with reality of front lines
description: A Russian exile media outlet has analyzed nearly 7,000 letters from Russian soldiers and their families to the Kremlin thanks to a security flaw.
